title: Agents, Workflows, and the Control Loop · Loop Forensics
summary: Compare a fixed workflow with an adaptive tool-using agent. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an agent-system boundary map and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish predefined workflows from model-directed agents and choose the simpler sufficient architecture.
Model the observe, decide, act, and verify cycle with explicit state transitions.
Separate model context, application state, external state, and durable records.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Loop Invariants evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Loop Invariants assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: State invariants that must hold before and after every loop iteration. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: loops foundations. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Loop Invariants, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an agent-system boundary map.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Only the final answer needs validation. This fails the Loop Invariants contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Tool Use with Claude (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls client and server tool loops, stop reasons, schemas, and tool choice.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State invariants that must hold before and after every loop iteration.

Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
